title: clinopodium troodi subsp. vardaranum
intro: Clinopodium troodi subsp. vardaranum, also known as Armenian mint or Vardan mint, is a subspecies of the Clinopodium troodi plant, which belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is a type of herbaceous perennial plant. It is native to the mountainous regions of Armenia and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of Clinopodium troodi subsp. vardaranum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, reduce fever, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used topically in the form of salves or creams to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
